steps.title: Identify the decisive data column
steps.content: The table includes a Percent Change in hours column. To compare declines across countries with different baseline hours worked in 1950, percent change is the standard measurement—not absolute hour changes. This immediately signals that choices C and D (which use percentages) are the candidates.

steps.title: Verify the percent comparison against the table
steps.content: Argentina's percent change is -17%. Compared to the other countries: less than Ireland (-28%) and the UK (-24%), but greater than Mexico (-3%)—exactly as choice D states. The other three choices either use absolute hour changes instead of percentages or get the percentage comparisons wrong. That matches choice D — D is correct.
